title: Machine Learning Engineer Intern
summary: Incentive decisioning and uplift modeling for the OneKey loyalty program on the Membership & Engagement team.
bullets: Built a real-time incentive decisioning service with FastAPI for the 98.6M-member OneKey loyalty program
Proved a +0.58pp causal booking lift from a $15 incentive via randomized holdout analysis of 1M+ members
Showed 94% of incentivized bookers were windfall using T-learner CATE, making the case for uplift targeting
Distilled 112 features to 27 servable for a two-tower reward recommender via a train/serve feature contract

title: BSCS, Data Science minor
summary: BSCS with a Data Science minor. Expected May 2027; transferred from UW-Madison after one year.

title: Software Engineer Intern
summary: Built backend APIs, test automation, and data tooling across a Spring Boot and AWS stack.
bullets: Built Java Spring Boot microservices that process and validate 120k accounting events, deployed via Jenkins CI/CD
Designed GraphQL schema and resolvers to consolidate 200+ REST endpoints, cutting Angular client payload by 60%
Led a POC comparing Playwright and Cypress, achieving 4x faster tests (8 to 2 minutes) and driving tool migration
Automated nightly data refresh via Python and Jenkins, cutting UAT load time from 2 hours to 10 minutes
Classified transactions with business rules, ingesting from AWS S3 and storing results in MongoDB

title: AI/ML Research Assistant
summary: Graph-based anomaly detection for financial crime research on GPU supercomputing infrastructure.
bullets: Built scalable graph-analysis pipelines with cuGraph, SciPy, and NetworkX on 1.2M-edge ICIJ and crypto graphs
Raised AUC-ROC from 0.55 (logistic baseline) to 0.76, surfacing 250 anomalies at 89% precision
Applied centrality metrics and One-Class SVM to flag and rank illicit transaction patterns
Scaled GPU-parallel anomaly detection to 2M+ nodes and edges with cuGraph
Validated the model on IBM AMLSim, confirming performance across real and synthetic financial graphs

title: Software Engineer Intern
summary: Built and shipped an AWS-hosted documentation portal with React and Docusaurus.
bullets: Built and deployed an AWS-hosted Docusaurus portal with React and Terraform, serving 500+ engineers
Integrated AWS CodeBuild to run lint and test checks on pull requests, reducing manual QA
Cut onboarding tickets 85% (90 to 13) in one month by redesigning site flow and UI after user testing
Added Algolia DocSearch: 95% top-3 result rate and 120ms median query latency

title: Started CS at UW-Madison
summary: Transferred to UVA after one year.
